tagged_story_for_tts: <[silence:3400ms]>"Only my mind exists. <[silence:1300ms]> Everything else? Unprovable.<[silence:1300ms]>'Solipsism' comes from Latin solus ('alone') and ipse ('self'), meaning only your own thoughts are certain.<[silence:700ms]>It’s like waking in a dream where every person, tree, and sound is just your brain’s invention—no way to check if they’re real.<[silence:1300ms]>Here’s the trap:<[silence:700ms]>You feel pain when you stub your toe—proof you exist.<[silence:700ms]>But the toe itself?<[silence:700ms]>Maybe just an idea in your mind.<[silence:700ms]>No test can prove otherwise.<[silence:1300ms]>Even science relies on shared observations—yet solipsism asks: What if ‘shared’ is another illusion?<[silence:700ms]>A famous thought experiment:<[silence:700ms]>If a ‘zombie’ acted human but had no inner life, could you ever know?<[silence:700ms]>Solipsism says no—because you might be the only one with a mind at all.<[silence:1300ms]>It’s not about loneliness.<[silence:700ms]>It’s about logic pushed to its extreme.<[silence:2000ms]>Three related subjects are perception limits, reality tests, and idealist philosophy.<[silence:1200ms]>
clean_story: "Only my mind exists.  Everything else? Unprovable. 'Solipsism' comes from Latin solus ('alone') and ipse ('self'), meaning only your own thoughts are certain. It’s like waking in a dream where every person, tree, and sound is just your brain’s invention—no way to check if they’re real. Here’s the trap:You feel pain when you stub your toe—proof you exist. But the toe itself?Maybe just an idea in your mind. No test can prove otherwise. Even science relies on shared observations—yet solipsism asks: What if ‘shared’ is another illusion?A famous thought experiment:If a ‘zombie’ acted human but had no inner life, could you ever know?Solipsism says no—because you might be the only one with a mind at all. It’s not about loneliness. It’s about logic pushed to its extreme. Three related subjects are perception limits, reality tests, and idealist philosophy.
